name: Puntal Blanco — circular desde el Embalse del Negratín
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Embalse del Negratín traverse a landscape characterized by its arid badlands, eroded formations, and the expansive turquoise waters of the reservoir. The region features a diverse terrain, transitioning from stark, desert-like areas with impressive "cárcavas" to sections with pine forests. Dominating the horizon, Cerro Jabalcón offers panoramic views, and the area is part of the Geopark of Granada, highlighting its geological significance. Best mountain bike trails around Embalse del Negratín The most popular mountain bike trail is Puntal Blanco — circular desde el Embalse del Negratín, an 18.1 miles (29.2 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 43 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding arid terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Gorafe Desert – Gorafe Desert Canyons loop from Central Hidroeléctrica de Negratín, a difficult 52.3 miles (84.1 km) path. This route explores the unique canyons and desert landscapes of the Gorafe Desert. Local mountain bikers also love the Puntal de Don Diego Viewpoint – Los Coloraos Badlands loop from Bácor, a 34.4 miles (55.3 km) trail leading through eroded badlands, often completed in about 5 hours 19 minutes. Mountain biking around Embalse del Negratín is defined by arid badlands, the vast turquoise reservoir, and unique eroded formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Embalse del Negratín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 60 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Embalse del Negratín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Embalse del Negratín?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Embalse del Negratín, offering a diverse range of experiences. Most routes are moderate to difficult, with one easy option available.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain is highly diverse, transitioning from arid, ochre-toned 'Badlands' with impressive eroded formations like 'cárcavas' and 'fairy chimneys' to sections with lush pine forests. You'll also encounter the expansive turquoise waters of the reservoir and views of Cerro Jabalcón.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails for beginners or families?
A: While most routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available. For a generally moderate experience, consider routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013409603/puntal-blanco-circular-desde-el-embalse-del-negratin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal Blanco — circular desde el Embalse del Negratín&lt;/a&gt;, which is 29.2 km long and takes about 2 hours 43 minutes.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes in the area?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, options include the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert – Gorafe Desert Canyons loop from Central Hidroeléctrica de Negratín&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 84.1 km, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1143313121/negratin-reservoir-loop-from-zujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Negratín Reservoir Loop from Zújar&lt;/a&gt;, a 71.4 km difficult route.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Embalse del Negratín?
A: The trails are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Riders often praise the unique landscapes, the dramatic contrast between the reservoir's blue waters and the arid badlands, and the challenging yet rewarding routes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Embalse del Negratín?
A: Yes, many routes are circular. A popular moderate circular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013409603/puntal-blanco-circular-desde-el-embalse-del-negratin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal Blanco — circular desde el Embalse del Negratín&lt;/a&gt;. For a more challenging loop, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24525189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de Don Diego Viewpoint – Los Coloraos Badlands loop from Bácor&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see or do besides mountain biking near Embalse del Negratín?
A: Beyond mountain biking, the area offers a variety of attractions. You can explore the unique eroded formations known as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6485845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoodoos of Cuevas del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, relax at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6585835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zújar Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5311664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Baths of Negratín&lt;/a&gt;. The reservoir itself, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Negratín Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, is great for water sports like kayaking and paddle surfing, and there are designated bathing zones.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Embalse del Negratín?
A: Given the arid nature of the region, spring and autumn are generally the best times for mountain biking. The temperatures are milder, making for more comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly, though often sunny.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. The trails offer breathtaking views, particularly of the reservoir's turquoise waters contrasting with the surrounding badlands. Cerro Jabalcón dominates the horizon and provides panoramic vistas. Routes often lead through impressive canyons and offer distant views of the Sierra Nevada mountains.

Q: Is Embalse del Negratín part of a protected area or geopark?
A: Yes, Embalse del Negratín is located within the newly established Geopark of Granada, highlighting its significant geological interest and unique natural features, including the impressive eroded badlands.

Q: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the mountain bike trails?
A: The surrounding villages, such as Freila and Zújar, offer cultural attractions like traditional cave houses and may have local cafes or accommodation options. It's advisable to check specific village amenities for services like bike rental or dining.
tips: Summit located on the border between Granada and Jaén, both provinces located in Andalusia. With its more than 870m high, it offers panoramic views of the surrounding desert mountains.

The Negratín reservoir was inaugurated in 1984 and its construction meant the disappearance of the town of Las Juntas and the old spa of Zujar, as well as various farmhouses that remained under the waters of the reservoir.
The Negratín reservoir is the second largest reservoir in the Autonomous Community of Andalusia, with more than 567 Hm3.
